params.text:
Module 2: Clinical Photography Best Practices

Capturing High-Quality Diagnostic Images

Image Quality Requirements

High-quality images are essential for AI training. Your photos should:

  • Be in focus - Sharp detail of lesions
  • Have good lighting - Natural or clinical light, avoid shadows
  • Show clinical context - Include surrounding skin for comparison
  • Be properly oriented - Correct anatomical positioning
  • Have minimal artifacts - No jewelry, clothing, or debris in frame

Important: All images will be de-identified before use in AI training.

Each submission must include:

FieldPurpose
Partner OrganizationAccountability and tracking
Submitting ClinicianAttribution and verification
CountryGeographic diversity for AI
Clinical Form (Ridley-Jopling)Classification for training
Operational ClassificationTreatment category
Reaction StatusPresence of reactions
GenderDemographic balance
Age GroupDemographic balance
Phototype (Fitzpatrick)Skin tone diversity
Anatomical LocationBody part shown in image
Image Capture MethodCamera type/quality info

DO:

  • Clean camera lens before use
  • Use macro mode for close-ups
  • Include ruler/coin for scale
  • Take multiple angles
  • Ensure even lighting
  • Photograph on neutral background
  • Check focus before submission

DON'T:

  • Use flash that creates glare
  • Include faces in photos
  • Capture identifying tattoos
  • Submit blurry images
  • Show jewelry or clothing
  • Photograph through bandages
  • Submit without checking

Private Body Areas:

When photographing lesions on breasts, genitals, buttocks, or inner thighs:

  • Obtain explicit specific consent for that body region
  • Use professional draping to expose only necessary area
  • Ensure complete privacy (closed door, minimal personnel)
  • Consider gender concordance when feasible
  • Offer chaperone of patient's choice
  • Work efficiently while maintaining dignity

Remember: Technical quality is important, but patient dignity is paramount.

Knowledge Check

Which metadata field is used to ensure skin tone diversity in the AI training dataset?

params.question: Which metadata field is used to ensure skin tone diversity in the AI training dataset?

answers.tip: Incorrect. Age Group tracks demographic age distribution, not skin tone.
Incorrect. This classifies the type/severity of leprosy.
Correct! The Fitzpatrick Scale (I-VI) documents skin tone to ensure AI works across diverse populations.
Incorrect. This categorizes paucibacillary vs multibacillary for treatment.
